name: Prefix Shrinking
summary: Start with the first string as the candidate prefix, then shrink it until each next string begins with it.

lineExplanations.3: Use the first string as the largest possible candidate prefix.
lineExplanations.4: Loop through every remaining string to validate the candidate prefix.
lineExplanations.5: Load the current string that must start with the candidate prefix.
lineExplanations.6: Check whether the current string starts with the current candidate prefix.
lineExplanations.7: Continue shrinking while the prefix is non-empty and does not match the current string start.
lineExplanations.8: Remove the last character from the candidate prefix.
lineExplanations.9: Recheck whether the shortened prefix now matches the start of the current string.
lineExplanations.10: Check whether all candidate characters have been removed.
lineExplanations.11: Store the empty string because no common prefix remains.
lineExplanations.12: Return immediately because an empty prefix cannot become non-empty later.
lineExplanations.13: Store the surviving prefix after all strings accepted it.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: defines the wrapper class expected by LeetCode and the tracer.
syntaxExplanations.2: def declares the method; self is the object instance and strs is the input list.
syntaxExplanations.3: strs[0] accesses the first string in the input list.
syntaxExplanations.4: range(1, len(strs)) creates indexes for every string after the first.
syntaxExplanations.5: s = strs[i] stores the current string in a shorter variable name.
syntaxExplanations.6: s[0:len(prefix)] takes the starting part of s with the same length as prefix, then == compares it to prefix.
syntaxExplanations.7: while repeats the indented body while prefix is non-empty and starts_ok is False.
syntaxExplanations.8: prefix[:-1] creates a new string without the last character.
syntaxExplanations.9: This assignment recalculates whether the shortened prefix matches the start of s.
syntaxExplanations.10: if prefix == "" checks whether the candidate prefix is empty.
syntaxExplanations.11: result = "" stores the empty string as the answer.
syntaxExplanations.12: return result exits the function immediately.
syntaxExplanations.13: result = prefix stores the final candidate prefix.
syntaxExplanations.14: return result exits the function with the answer.
